titleStmt.title: SII 11.96: original edition by C. R. Krishnamacharlu, R. S. Panchamukhi, N. Lakshminaraya Rao and H. K. Narasimhaswami
No. 96. (B.K. No. 37 of 1928-29). ARASĪBĪḌI, HUNGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT. ON A SLAB STANDING ON A HILL TO THE RIGHT OF THE JAIN BASTI.

summary.p: This inscription belongs to Trailōkyamalladēva (Sōmēśvara I). It records that Mahāpradhāna (and) Daṇḍanāyaka Cheṭṭimayya who managed the Vaḍḍarāvuḷa revenue made with the sanction of the emperor a gift of one paṇa and 100 areca-nuts on every areca-palm garden to the Forty-two (Mahājanas) of the Brahmapurī at Vikramapura (modern Arasībīḍi). The inscription is engraved below a record of Vikramāditya VI (No. 36 of 1928-29) on the same slab and must therefore be a later copy.

The record is dated Śaka 982, Śārvari, Pushya, puṇṇami, Monday, lunar eclipse. The details regularly correspond to A.D. 1061, January 8, Monday; ·77, when there was a lunar eclipse. The lunar month was Māgha and not Pushya as quoted in the inscription.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
© svasti samastabhuvanāśraya śrīpṛthvīvallabha mahārājādhirāja paramēśvara parama baṭṭāraka satyāśra
yakuḷatiḷaka cāḷukyābharaṇa śrīma ttraiḷōkyamalladēvara vijayarājya muttarōttarābhivṛ
ddhi pravarddhamāna mācaṁdrārkkatāraṁ saluttamire tatpādapadmōpajīvi svasti samastapraśastisahitaṁ śrī
manmahāpradhānaṁ daṇḍanāyakaṁ ceṭṭimayyaṁ vaḍḍarāvuḷa sunkamaṁ sukhasaṁkathāvinōdadinā
ḷuttamiḻdu sakavarṣa 982 neya śārvvari saṁvatsarada pupyada puṇnami sōmavāra sōma
grahaṇamādaṁdu cakravarttige binnapaṁgeydu vikramapurada brahmapuriya nālva
ttirvvargge suṁkamaṁ māṇdu māḍida mārggamentene Aḍake tōṁṭakke paṇa voṁdu
Aḍake nūṟu Ī dharmmamaṁ svadharmmadiṁ rakṣisidavaru vāraṇāsiya loṁdu
kōṭi kavileya nanibaru vēdapāragarappa brāhmaṇargge koṭṭa phalama ne
yduvaru Ida naḷidava rāsthānada lanitu kavileyu brāhmaṇaruma naḷida dōṣama
neyduvaru .
Digital edition of SII 11.96 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
